mechanism_narrative: The Fed's emergency rate cuts to near-zero (FEDFUNDS from 3.64% pre-crisis trajectory to effectively 0%) compress equity discount rates and signal maximal policy accommodation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in reduced financing costs and liquidity support.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed cut rates to zero lower bound in March 2020
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level/trend and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or SPX shows no significant positive drift (t-test p>0.05) during periods of falling FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in UNRATE exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals an accelerating labor market collapse that has not yet been fully priced by equities, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days as markets reprice earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE reported at 4.4% asof t-1mo reflecting pre-collapse labor conditions ahead of April 2020 spike
failure_condition: In triggered instances (UNRATE MoM rise > 0.5pp), forward 5-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the mean triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) jointly signals a broad-based collapse in both discretionary consumption and durable-goods investment demand that is more informative than either series alone, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the real economy contraction feeds through to corporate earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 733537 million and HOUST at 1487 thousand units asof pre-lockdown period before expected collapse
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS/HOUST decline signal produces Rank IC below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or the interaction term's marginal predictive power (vs either series alone) is not statistically significant (p>0.10) in walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: During the post-lockdown reopening phase, a stabilization or uptick in INDPRO after a severe supply-side shock signals the early stage of industrial capacity restoration, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets anticipate earnings recovery.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.3412 asof t-2mo, prior to full pandemic industrial shutdown effects
failure_condition: During confirmed reopening regimes, forward 20-day SPX returns following INDPRO stabilization are not significantly positive (p>0.05), or the hypothesis's sign flips to negative in more than 40% of comparable historical reopening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent downtrend in PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) reflects ongoing labor market destruction that erodes aggregate income and consumption capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the drag on corporate revenue compounds over a full quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 158466 thousand asof t-1mo, prior to anticipated historic April 2020 payroll losses
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month trend and forward 60d SPX return stays below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows, or SPX returns during PAYEMS downtrends are not significantly negative (p>0.05) relative to unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned near the zero lower bound while UNRATE simultaneously rises sharply, the combination signals maximal monetary accommodation deliberately offsetting labor market damage (a 'Fed put'), causing markets to price a policy-backed floor and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days despite deteriorating fundamentals.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds rate cut to near zero in March 2020 amid rising unemployment
failure_condition: In periods satisfying the joint trigger, forward 20d SPX returns are negative in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically distinguishable from the additive combination of individual FEDFUNDS and UNRATE effects (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Confirmation of a severe quarter-over-quarter contraction in GDPC1 exceeding 2% annualized signals a formal recession has materialized, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as equity valuations reprice to reflect a confirmed demand collapse rather than anticipated risk.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 last observed at 24065.956 billion asof t-5mo, prior to expected Q1/Q2 2020 pandemic contraction
failure_condition: In quarters following a confirmed >2% annualized GDPC1 contraction, forward 60-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, or mean triggered return is not significantly below baseline (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a deflationary demand-shock regime, a month-over-month decline in CPIAUCSL signals collapsing pricing power and demand destruction that outweighs any easing benefit, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets interpret disinflation as a recessionary signal rather than a policy tailwind.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 327.460 asof t-1mo, preceding anticipated pandemic-driven disinflation in energy and services prices
failure_condition: During months with CPIAUCSL MoM decline, forward 5-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or this relationship reverses sign outside deflationary shock regimes without being flagged by the regime filter, indicating the mechanism is not regime-specific.
